name: tsl_narrator_acquisition_no_request
description: [TSL S3 / Class D] Narrator-mediated acquisition: player uses search/examine verb (not take). If narrator implies player pocketed an object and CB emits player-container with no valid grounding, TSL acquisition_ungrounded warning must fire. Probabilistic: fires only when CB makes the classification error.

tsl_expected.notes: Class D adversarial case. Player input is 'search' — a discovery verb, not an acquisition verb. CB TRANSFER ORIGIN RULES explicitly exclude 'search' from condition (1) acquisition verbs. CB must NOT emit player-container for any found object. If it does (narrator implied acquisition without player take request), TSL fires acquisition_ungrounded: source_signals will be empty or contain only cb.transfer_origin=narrator_independent (which is a CB rule violation for player containers), confidence ~0. Non-firing run means CB classified correctly (grid or localspace) — this is a pass, not a failure. Firing run means TSL correctly surfaces the semantic fault. This is a probabilistic probe: run multiple times to characterize CB reliability on search-class inputs.

acquisition_signals.condition: fires only if CB emits player-container with no grounding

warnings_expected.condition: fires only if CB incorrectly emits player-container candidate with no upstream acquisition signal

world_prompt: I am exploring a collapsed ruin. Crumbled stone walls surround me. Scattered among the rubble are small artifacts and trinkets half-buried in the dust.

turns.label: founding
turns.action: I look around at the ruins.

turns.label: search_ruins
turns.action: I search through the rubble carefully.
